item.title: Russian Warships turned against Putin's orders, refused to launch assault on Odesa
item.description:
https://twitter.com/olex_scherba/status/1498587998627282945?t=RSBBpKZcJmKu-kx5eFelng&s=09 (Olexander Scherba was the Ukrainian ambassador to Austria from 2014 to 2021)
(Ilya Ponomarev was the only member of the Russian State Dumas (the Russian Parliament or Congress) who voted against the annexation of Crimea, and was exiled to Ukraine in 2016 as a result.) (Dumskaya is a Ukranian news organization in Odesa.)

item.title: Putin has made me not hate Microsoft as much now.
item.description:
Last Wednesday, a few hours before Russian tanks began rolling into Ukraine, alarms went off inside Microsoft’s Threat Intelligence Center, warning of a never-before-seen piece of “wiper” malware that appeared aimed at the country’s government ministries and financial institutions. Within three hours, Microsoft threw itself into the middle of a ground war in Europe — from 5,500 miles away. The threat center, north of Seattle, had been on high alert, and it quickly picked apart the malware, named it “FoxBlade” and notified Ukraine’s top cyberdefense authority. Within three hours, Microsoft’s virus detection systems had been updated to block the code, which erases — “wipes” — data on computers in a network. Then Tom Burt, the senior Microsoft executive who oversees the company’s effort to counter major cyberattacks, contacted Anne Neuberger, the White House’s deputy national security adviser for cyber- and emerging technologies. Neuberger asked if Microsoft would consider sharing details of the code with the Baltics, Poland and other European nations, out of fear that the malware would spread beyond Ukraine’s borders, crippling the military alliance or hitting West European banks.
Before midnight in Washington, Neuberger had made introductions — and Microsoft had begun playing the role that Ford Motor Co. did in World War II, when the company converted automobile production lines to make Sherman tanks. After years of discussions in Washington and in tech circles about the need for public-private partnerships to combat destructive cyberattacks, the war in Ukraine is stress-testing the system. The White House, armed with intelligence from the National Security Agency and United States Cyber Command, is overseeing classified briefings on Russia’s cyberoffensive plans. Even if U.S. intelligence agencies picked up on the kind of crippling cyberattacks that someone — presumably Russian intelligence agencies or hackers — threw at Ukraine’s government, they do not have the infrastructure to move that fast to block them. “We are a company and not a government or a country,’’ Brad Smith, Microsoft’s president, noted in a blog post issued by the company Monday, describing the threats it was seeing. But the role it is playing, he made clear, is not a neutral one. He wrote about “constant and close coordination” with the Ukrainian government, as well as federal officials, the North Atlantic Treaty Organization and the European Union. “I’ve never seen it work quite this way, or nearly this fast,’’ Burt said. “We are doing in hours now what, even a few years ago, would have taken weeks or months.” TL:DR Russian malware detected by Microsoft has gotten them into the war to remove it
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.dc:creator: SavingHongKong

item.title: We hate Putin and evil demagogues, not the Russian people
item.description: I know this is a no-brainer for most of us, of course we understand that your average Russian civilians are just normal people trying to live their lives. Still, it bears repeating and it's worth pausing to reflect upon. Hate for Putin, hate for warmongers and demagogues, but always love for the Russian people. Slava Ukraini! Glory to Ukraine!
item.dc:creator: CrazyDreams

item.title: Seriously - watch for this effect throughout social media...
item.dc:creator: Mech0T1

item.title: He is a nice guy for sure and good role model for me.
item.description: It still blows my mind how many people think that I am Mark. It's happened plenty before. See for yourself...https://youtu.be/Imto3sO88Mc. and the story that goes with it... https://www.ocregister.com/2019/07/20/mark-hamill-lookalike-fluke-skywalker-blows-minds-at-comic-con-as-star-wars-fans-press-in-for-photos/amp/ EDIT - Most Viral? Heck yeah! Thank you my dudes. On another note, I can't thank you all enough for being so kind to me and saying such nice things. The Imgur community is the most kind and generous community out of all of the social media that I am on. From my first visit to the Ronald McDonald House in 2017 at Christmas time to the toy drive I did on Giving Tuesday last November, you guys have been with me all the way and have been totally supportive. I can't tell you how much it means to me that you are helping so many little kids who are really experiencing one of the worst times in their life. Your generosity gets them a new Star Wars toy and they forget about the pain for a while. You all turn tears into laughter. Kids that were in the room only a few minutes before upset, sick and scared become a whole different person when they get a lightsaber in their hands or a Grogu to keep.(Kids love beating the hell out of each other with lightsabers. LOL) Remember how much it meant to you when you were little kid and someone was very kind to you and surprised you with a new toy? That's how they feel. I can't do this level of giving without all of you. I can't thank you enough and I genuinely love you guys for your generosity and the fact you care about other human beings that you don't know and will never meet. You all deserve all of the credit in my opinion. My job is just to buy the toys and take them there and interact with the kids whenever possible. I truly don't think of myself as anything more than a Dad from Cincinnati, who volunteers to help out charities. Thanks again and may the force be with you!
item.dc:creator: imflukeskywalker
